title: Euodia knudsenii
intro: Euodia knudsenii, also known as Knudsen's Euodia or Yuzu acacia, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Euodia knudsenii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Euodia knudsenii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, sore throats, and coughs.
taxonomy: Euodia knudsenii is classified in the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This plant belongs to the genus Euodia, which includes several species of flowering plants native to Asia. The genus Euodia is further classified under the order Sapindales, which includes other plants such as citrus and mango. The family Rutaceae and the order Sapindales are part of the larger clade of angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. The classification of Euodia knudsenii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rutaceae family.
